Sprint.write_rule: Fetch live Project iterations before writing.

agent_owned_namespace.authorization: Allowed inside an authorized agent-owned workflow.

unknown_label_policy.handling: Report old or unknown labels in an audit or cleanup dry run. Do not create, apply, or recreate labels that are not listed in this taxonomy.

canonical_labels.name: feature
canonical_labels.description: PR adds or expands user-visible capability.

canonical_labels.name: refactor
canonical_labels.description: PR restructures code without intended behavior change.

canonical_labels.name: chore
canonical_labels.description: Docs, CI, dependencies, packaging, policy, or maintenance.

canonical_labels.name: security
canonical_labels.description: Potential vulnerability, unsafe behavior, or access risk.
negative_signals: ordinary UX issue without security impact

canonical_labels.name: PRR
canonical_labels.description: Reserved Product Readiness Review label for reports or follow-up used to assess product readiness and user experience.
positive_signals: product readiness assessment tied to a PRR
user experience assessment tied to a PRR
negative_signals: daily release label or readiness claim
canonical_labels.application_policy: Reserved for maintainers or dedicated PRR workflows. Triage agents must not recommend or apply this label during normal triage.

canonical_labels.name: needs: triage
canonical_labels.description: Existing inbox/placeholder signal for unprocessed items; normal triage agents should not newly add it.
positive_signals: pre-triage inbox state before an agent produces recommendations

canonical_labels.name: needs: info
canonical_labels.description: Waiting on author for missing details required before work can proceed.

canonical_labels.name: needs: design
positive_signals: cross-cutting change with no clear owner
product decision needed before work can proceed

canonical_labels.name: needs: rebase
canonical_labels.description: PR needs rebase or conflict resolution.

canonical_labels.name: needs: unblock
canonical_labels.description: Blocked item needs dependency or decision resolved.

canonical_labels.name: needs: cleanup-review
canonical_labels.description: Stale, superseded, competing, convergence-needed, or closure-candidate item needs maintainer judgment.

canonical_labels.name: good first issue
canonical_labels.description: Small, clear, safe task for new contributors with tiny blast radius and no permission, secret, security, release, or policy risk.

canonical_labels.name: help wanted
canonical_labels.description: Accepted work where maintainers welcome external contribution.

selection_guidance: Use area labels for the affected product or code surface, not for every concept mentioned in the report.

For install/onboarding/packaging overlap: use area: install for prerequisites or setup mechanics, area: onboarding for first-run flow and onboarding state, and area: packaging for shipped artifacts, images, registries, or distribution.

For inference/providers/routing/local-models overlap: use area: inference for model execution or output behavior, area: providers for provider integration/configuration/selection work, area: routing for dispatch/fallback/model-selection logic, and area: local-models for local runtime, download, launch, or connectivity.

For integrations/messaging overlap: use area: integrations for external app or bridge behavior, area: messaging when message delivery/channel lifecycle is the affected subsystem, and add a specific integration:* label when one listed integration is involved.
Do not use only area: integrations when a canonical integration is named or clearly implicated; include the matching integration:* label.

For CI/e2e overlap: use area: ci for workflow, check, release automation, nightly-runner, or test infrastructure failures. Do not add area: ci merely because an e2e failure was observed in CI; use both area: ci and area: e2e only when the CI workflow, runner, scheduling, logs, or test infrastructure is part of the affected surface.

entries.name: area: architecture
entries.description: Architecture, design debt, major refactors, or maintainability.

entries.name: area: ci
entries.description: CI workflows, checks, release automation, or GitHub Actions.
negative_signals: e2e failure merely observed in CI
runtime bug found by CI but not caused by CI

entries.name: area: cli
entries.description: Command line interface, flags, terminal UX, or output.
negative_signals: backend behavior with no CLI-specific surface

entries.name: area: docs
entries.description: Documentation, examples, guides, generated docs, or docs build.

entries.name: area: e2e
entries.description: End-to-end tests, nightly failures, or validation infrastructure.
negative_signals: single component failure outside e2e flow

entries.name: area: inference
entries.description: Inference routing, serving, model selection, or generated outputs.

entries.name: area: install
entries.description: Install, setup, prerequisites, or uninstall flow.

entries.name: area: integrations
entries.description: External app, tool, channel, or OpenClaw integration behavior.
negative_signals: core product behavior with no external integration
specific canonical integration named but matching integration:* label omitted

entries.name: area: local-models
entries.description: Local model providers, downloads, launch, or connectivity.
negative_signals: generic inference issue without local runtime evidence

entries.name: area: messaging
entries.description: Messaging channels, bridges, manifests, or channel lifecycle.

entries.name: area: networking
entries.description: DNS, proxy, TLS, ports, host aliases, or connectivity.
negative_signals: provider outage with no local networking evidence

entries.name: area: observability
entries.description: Logging, metrics, tracing, diagnostics, or debug output.
negative_signals: logs only included as evidence for a different issue

entries.name: area: onboarding
entries.description: First-run, onboarding FSM, provider setup, or sandbox launch.

entries.name: area: packaging
entries.description: Packages, images, registries, installers, or distribution.
negative_signals: runtime code bug unrelated to packaging

entries.name: area: performance
entries.description: Latency, throughput, resource use, benchmarks, or scaling.

entries.name: area: policy
entries.description: Network policy, egress rules, presets, or sandbox policy.

entries.name: area: project-management
entries.description: Taxonomy, triage, workflow, roadmap, or project process.

entries.name: area: providers
entries.description: Inference provider integration, configuration, or selection work.
negative_signals: routing-only issue with no provider configuration or adapter behavior
entries.companion_label_guidance: Add provider:* when a listed provider is specifically involved. Use area: providers without provider:* for unknown, proposed, or non-canonical providers.

entries.name: area: routing
entries.description: Request routing, policy routing, model selection, or fallback logic.
negative_signals: single provider failure with no routing behavior

entries.name: area: sandbox
entries.description: OpenShell sandbox lifecycle, runtime, configuration, or recovery.
negative_signals: general container issue not tied to sandbox behavior

entries.name: area: security
entries.description: Security controls, permissions, secrets, or hardening.

entries.name: area: skills
entries.description: Agent skills, prompts, behaviors, or skill packaging.
negative_signals: ordinary user workflow without agent or skill behavior

entries.name: area: ui
entries.description: Web UI, terminal display, visual layout, or UX behavior.
negative_signals: backend behavior with no visible user interface impact

selection_guidance: Apply platform labels only when the platform appears causal, routing-relevant, or materially more likely to own the issue.
Do not apply a platform label just because the platform appears in the environment template, reproduction setup, or logs.
When a more specific platform label applies, prefer it over a broader one unless both are independently routing-relevant.

entries.name: platform: arm64
entries.description: ARM64 or aarch64-specific behavior.
positive_signals: reproduction differs between ARM64 and x86_64
negative_signals: Apple Silicon or Jetson issue better routed by platform: macos or platform: jetson alone

entries.name: platform: brev
entries.description: Brev hosted development environments.
negative_signals: cloud development environment without Brev evidence

entries.name: platform: container
entries.description: Docker, containerd, Podman, or image behavior.
positive_signals: image pull or image build failure
negative_signals: container is only part of generic setup and not likely causal

entries.name: platform: dgx-spark
entries.description: DGX Spark hardware or workflows.
negative_signals: NVIDIA hardware mentioned without DGX Spark relevance

entries.name: platform: dgx-station
entries.description: DGX Station hardware or workflows.
negative_signals: NVIDIA hardware mentioned without DGX Station relevance

entries.name: platform: gb10
entries.description: GB10 GPU environments.
negative_signals: generic GPU issue without GB10 evidence

entries.name: platform: jetson
entries.description: Jetson AGX Thor or Orin environments.

entries.name: platform: k3s
entries.description: K3s-specific behavior.
negative_signals: generic Kubernetes issue better labeled platform: k8s

entries.name: platform: k8s
entries.description: Kubernetes-specific behavior.

entries.name: platform: linux
entries.description: Linux behavior without Ubuntu specificity.

entries.name: platform: macos
entries.description: macOS, Darwin, Homebrew, or Apple Silicon behavior.
negative_signals: macOS only listed as tested environment
same issue reproduced on Linux or Windows

entries.name: platform: ubuntu
entries.description: Ubuntu-specific behavior.

entries.name: platform: windows
entries.description: Native Windows or PowerShell behavior.
negative_signals: Windows host only listed in environment

entries.name: platform: wsl
entries.description: Windows Subsystem for Linux behavior.
negative_signals: generic Linux issue without WSL-specific evidence

positive_signals: platform seems routing-relevant or likely causal
negative_signals: logs mention platform without platform-specific behavior

selection_guidance: Apply provider labels only when the item is specific to that provider's API, configuration, runtime, endpoint, auth, model behavior, or adapter.
When a provider exposes an OpenAI-compatible API but has its own canonical provider label, use the more specific provider label instead of provider: openai.
Use area: providers with no provider:* label for unknown, proposed, or non-canonical providers.

entries.name: provider: anthropic
entries.description: Anthropic or Claude provider behavior.

entries.name: provider: nvidia
entries.description: NVIDIA inference endpoint, NIM, or NVIDIA provider behavior.

entries.name: provider: ollama
entries.description: Ollama local model provider behavior.
negative_signals: local model issue not using Ollama

entries.name: provider: openai
entries.description: OpenAI API or OpenAI-compatible provider behavior.
positive_signals: OpenAI-compatible endpoint with no more specific provider label
negative_signals: NVIDIA, Ollama, or vLLM issue with a more specific provider label

entries.name: provider: vllm
entries.description: vLLM local or hosted provider behavior.
negative_signals: generic local model issue not tied to vLLM

selection_guidance: Apply integration labels only when a listed external app, channel, tool, or agent integration is specifically involved.
Use area: integrations for integration subsystem work, and add integration:* when a listed integration is named or clearly implicated.

Use area: messaging when delivery, channel lifecycle, manifests, or bridge messages are the affected subsystem; combine it with integration:* when the messaging issue is specific to a listed integration.

If the title, body, linked issue, test name, file path, or PR prefix names a canonical integration as the affected subject, include the corresponding integration:* label. Do not replace the specific label with only area: integrations.

entries.name: integration: brave
entries.description: Brave integration behavior.
negative_signals: generic browser issue without Brave evidence

entries.name: integration: discord
entries.description: Discord bridge or channel lifecycle.
negative_signals: generic messaging issue without Discord evidence

entries.name: integration: hermes
entries.description: Hermes startup, plugin, sandbox, TUI, or Hermes model/tool-call behavior.
negative_signals: generic plugin issue without Hermes evidence

entries.name: integration: openclaw
entries.description: OpenClaw runtime, TUI, e2e tests, stubs, plugins, configuration, or bridge.
negative_signals: NemoClaw core behavior without OpenClaw involvement

entries.name: integration: slack
entries.description: Slack bridge, manifest, auth, or delivery behavior.
negative_signals: generic messaging issue without Slack evidence

entries.name: integration: telegram
entries.description: Telegram bot, bridge, polling, or delivery.
negative_signals: generic messaging issue without Telegram evidence

entries.name: integration: wechat
entries.description: WeChat channel or bridge behavior.
negative_signals: generic messaging issue without WeChat evidence

entries.name: integration: whatsapp
entries.description: WhatsApp channel setup or runtime.
negative_signals: generic messaging issue without WhatsApp evidence

positive_signals: maintainer selected PR for daily work
open PR activated for day queue
issue tracked as daily release attention signal
issue flagged as needing PR work for daily release
